raw_text:
ERROR   [TC-B-001] interface.f90:4 <-> header.h:2  param[0] 'n'
  TYPE_WIDTH: Fortran INTEGER(c_int) = 4 bytes. C 'long' = 8 bytes (LP64).
  Caller places 8 bytes; callee reads 4 bytes. High 32 bits are garbage.

ERROR   [TC-B-001] interface.f90:4 <-> header.h:2  param[1] 'nnz'
  TYPE_WIDTH: Same mismatch. Array bounds corrupted for nnz > 2^31-1.

ERROR   [TC-B-001] interface.f90:4 <-> header.h:2  param[3] 'col_idx'
  TYPE_WIDTH: Array element INTEGER(c_int) vs C 'long'. Every array
  element is 4 vs 8 bytes. Complete memory layout mismatch.

NOTE: Use c_long (Fortran) / int (C, explicitly) or c_int64_t / int64_t
      for unambiguous 64-bit indices.
